params.name: Jordanian Codices
params.description:
On Apr 7, 70 AD, after the fall of Jerusalem, a fleeing Christian hid the Jordan Codices in a remote cave.
The Jordanian Codices are early Christian documents about the size of a credit card, with over 70 pages held together by wire. They contain symbols, drawings, and writings, even detailing the story of Jesus' crucifixion and resurrection. Scholars believe that this book is the document referred to in the Book of Revelation and can help people better understand the early development of Chr …
